course_title: Developmental Perspectives on the Origins of Human Culture

learning_outcomes.description: Understand classic and contemporary literature on the evolution of human cultural cognition
Critically appraise research and theory on the evolution of human cultural cognition.
Have a flexible notion of the evolutionary origins of human culture
Apply theoretical perspectives on social and observational learning to a wide variety of research questions
Design and conduct research on issues relating to the evolution of human cultural cognition.

assessments.due_date:
4/08/2026 - 29/10/2026

Each test will open at 12pm each Tuesday and close at 12pm the Thursday immediately following (i.e., there is a 48 hour window to log-on and complete the quiz).
assessments.task_description: These are short on-line tests consisting of 8 Multiple Choice Questions (worth 0.5 each) designed to tap content from previous weeks (lectures and tutorials). They take place weekly from Week 2 through to Week 13 inclusive. In each week of the test, you will be able to log into Blackboard to access the test between 12PM on Tuesday and 12PM on Thursday. Once you open the test on Blackboard, you will have 40 minutes to complete the test. Please note that each test will only be available for 48 hours. Tests must be submitted BEFORE Thursday 12PM sharp in each week of the test. Once you start you WILL NOT be able to re-start. There are 12 tests in total with your best 11 counting towards your final mark.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description: Students will present a version of a PechaKucha 12x20 in which they will summarise a paper (allocated in tutorials) using 12 slides (+ a title and end slide), each of which are displayed for 20 seconds each and must advance automatically.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: If you are unable to complete your presentation you should submit your appropriate documentation (as per the extension policy) to your tutor and/or course coordinator to arrange for an alternate time to complete your presentation.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.due_date:
3/08/2026 - 30/10/2026

You will complete each quiz during tutorial classes.
assessments.task_description: Each quiz will consist of a Short Answer Question (worth 5%) covering preceding lectures and tutorials. This is a paper and pencil quiz completed during tutorial class. You will get 15 minutes to complete it. There are 9 quizzes of which 8 will count to your final mark (you may choose to omit doing 1 or sit all 9 and drop the lowest score).
assessments.submission_guidelines: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
